results_cost_benefit_description: Projected net savings over the next 10 years.
results_savings_breakdown_description: Percentage of water demand met by rainwater.

settings_app_info_description: Details about the application and its features.
settings_about_app_description: Rain2ground is an intelligent rainwater harvesting solution designed to help you conserve water and save money. Our platform provides personalized calculations based on your location and property details to estimate your harvesting potential.
footer_generated_by: Generated by Rain2ground - Intelligent Rainwater Harvesting Solutions.
settings_history_description: View your past calculations and reports.
settings_history_none: You have no saved calculations yet. Perform a calculation on the home page to see it here.
pie_chart_no_data: Not enough data to display chart.
forgot_password_step1_description: Enter your email to reset your password.
forgot_password_user_not_found_descripti: No account found with this email address.
forgot_password_success_description: Your password has been changed successfully.
toast_history_deleted_description: The calculation has been removed from your history.
feedback_dialog_description: Have a bug to report or a feature to suggest? We'd love to hear from you.
feedback_message_placeholder: Describe the issue or your suggestion...
feedback_success_description: Thank you for your feedback! We appreciate your input.
feedback_page_description: A collection of all feedback submitted by users.
feedback_page_no_feedback: No feedback has been submitted yet.
iot_connect_description: Follow these steps to connect your IoT sensor and stream live data to the app.
iot_firebase_config_description: Use this configuration object in your device's code to connect to your Firebase project.
iot_device_setup_description: To send data to the app, your device needs to be authenticated and write to the correct database path.
iot_step1: Set up your device with the Firebase SDK (e.g., for Arduino, ESP32, etc.).
iot_step2: Authenticate the device with Firebase. You can use a service account for secure, long-term access, or start with user email/password for testing.
iot_step3: Send your sensor data (e.g., rainfall in mm) to the Firebase Realtime Database under the path: iot_data/{user_id}/live_rainfall, where {user_id} is the Firebase Authentication UID of the logged-in user.
iot_example_code_description: Here is a basic example using the Firebase ESP Client library to send data.
